        // Track vanilla mode button reference
        private static UnityEngine.UI.Button _vanillaModeButton = null;
        private static UnityEngine.UI.Image _vanillaModeButtonImage = null;
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Create the Vanilla Mode toggle button next to the mouse toggle
        /// </summary>
        private void CreateVanillaModeButton()
        {
            var stationpedia = Stationpedia.Instance;
            if (stationpedia == null) return;
            
            // Find the ToggleMouse to position our button next to it
            var toggleMouse = stationpedia.ToggleMouse;
            if (toggleMouse == null)
            {
                Log?.LogWarning("ToggleMouse not found, cannot create vanilla mode button");
                return;
            }
            
            // Get the parent of the toggle to add our button as a sibling
            var parent = toggleMouse.transform.parent;
            if (parent == null) return;
            
            // Check if button already exists (prevent duplicates)
            var existingButton = parent.Find("VanillaModeButton");
            if (existingButton != null)
            {
                // Button already exists, get references and ensure click handler is attached
                _vanillaModeButton = existingButton.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.Button>();
                _vanillaModeButtonImage = existingButton.GetComponent<UnityEngine.UI.Image>();
                // Re-add click handler in case it was lost
                _vanillaModeButton.onClick.RemoveAllListeners();
                _vanillaModeButton.onClick.AddListener(OnVanillaModeButtonClicked);
                UpdateVanillaModeButtonAppearance();
                return;
            }
            
            // Create button GameObject
            var buttonGO = new GameObject("VanillaModeButton");
            buttonGO.transform.SetParent(parent, false);
            
            // Move it to be 1 position after the mouse toggle (to the right)
            int targetIndex = toggleMouse.transform.GetSiblingIndex() + 1;
            buttonGO.transform.SetSiblingIndex(targetIndex);
            
            // Add RectTransform
            var rt = buttonGO.AddComponent<RectTransform>();
            rt.sizeDelta = new Vector2(24, 24);
            
            // Add Image with book icon
            _vanillaModeButtonImage = buttonGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.Image>();
            if (_iconCollapsed != null)
            {
                _vanillaModeButtonImage.sprite = _iconCollapsed;  // Use book icon
                _vanillaModeButtonImage.preserveAspect = true;
            }
            _vanillaModeButtonImage.color = new Color(1f, 0.6f, 0.2f, 1f);  // More orange, less red for Ascended mode
            
            // Add Button component
            _vanillaModeButton = buttonGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.Button>();
            _vanillaModeButton.targetGraphic = _vanillaModeButtonImage;
            
            // Set up button colors
            var colors = _vanillaModeButton.colors;
            colors.normalColor = Color.white;
            colors.highlightedColor = new Color(0.8f, 0.8f, 0.8f, 1f);
            colors.pressedColor = new Color(0.6f, 0.6f, 0.6f, 1f);
            colors.selectedColor = Color.white;
            _vanillaModeButton.colors = colors;
            
            // Add click handler
            _vanillaModeButton.onClick.AddListener(OnVanillaModeButtonClicked);
            
            // Add LayoutElement to control size in layout group
            var layoutElement = buttonGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.LayoutElement>();
            layoutElement.preferredWidth = 24;
            layoutElement.preferredHeight = 24;
            layoutElement.minWidth = 24;
            layoutElement.minHeight = 24;
            
            Log?.LogInfo("Vanilla Mode button created successfully");
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Handle vanilla mode button click
        /// </summary>
        private void OnVanillaModeButtonClicked()
        {
            UI.VanillaModeManager.Toggle();
            UpdateVanillaModeButtonAppearance();
            
            // Force refresh the current page to apply new styling
            RefreshCurrentPage();
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Update the button appearance based on current mode
        /// </summary>
        private void UpdateVanillaModeButtonAppearance()
        {
            if (_vanillaModeButtonImage == null) return;
            
            if (UI.VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode)
            {
                _vanillaModeButtonImage.color = Color.white;  // White for Vanilla mode
            }
            else
            {
                _vanillaModeButtonImage.color = new Color(1f, 0.6f, 0.2f, 1f);  // More orange, less red for Ascended
            }
        }

        /// <summary>
        /// Update header appearance based on current mode
        /// </summary>
        private void UpdateHeaderAppearance()
        {
            if (_headerTitleText != null)
            {
                if (UI.VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode)
                {
                    // Vanilla mode - just "Stationpedia"
                    _headerTitleText.text = "Stationpedia";
                }
                else
                {
                    // Ascended mode - "Stationpedia Ascended" with orange styling
                    _headerTitleText.text = "Stationpedia <color=#FF7A18>Ascended</color>";
                }
            }
            
            if (_headerIconImage != null)
            {
                if (UI.VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode)
                {
                    // Restore original sprite and color in vanilla mode
                    if (_originalHeaderIconSprite != null)
                    {
                        _headerIconImage.sprite = _originalHeaderIconSprite;
                    }
                    _headerIconImage.color = Color.white;
                }
                else
                {
                    // Custom sprite with orange tint in Ascended mode
                    if (_customIconSprite != null)
                    {
                        _headerIconImage.sprite = _customIconSprite;
                        _headerIconImage.preserveAspect = true;
                    }
                    _headerIconImage.color = new Color(1f, 0.6f, 0.2f, 1f);
                }
            }
        }
```

        private void UpdateHeaderAppearance()

            if (_headerTitleText != null)

                if (UI.VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode)
                {
                    // Vanilla mode - just "Stationpedia"
                    _headerTitleText.text = "Stationpedia";
                }
                else
                {
                    // Ascended mode - "Stationpedia Ascended" with orange styling

            if (_headerIconImage != null)

                else

        /// <summary>
        /// Recursively build TOC links from operational details
        /// </summary>
        private static void BuildTocLinks(System.Text.StringBuilder sb, OperationalDetail detail, ref int index, int depth = 0)
        {
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(detail.tocId) && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(detail.title))
            {
                string indent = depth > 0 ? new string(' ', depth * 3) : "";
                // Use dash for sub-items, no bullet for top-level
                string bullet = depth > 0 ? "- " : "";
                // Use VanillaModeManager for colors - grey bullets, white text
                string bulletColor = VanillaModeManager.IsVanillaMode ? "#888888" : "#CC6600";
                string textColor = "#FFFFFF";  // Always white text
                if (depth > 0)
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"{indent}<color={bulletColor}>{bullet}</color><link=toc_{detail.tocId}><color={textColor}>{detail.title}</color></link>");
                }
                else
                {
                    sb.AppendLine($"<link=toc_{detail.tocId}><color={textColor}>{detail.title}</color></link>");
                }
                index++;
            }
            
            if (detail.children != null)
            {
                foreach (var child in detail.children)
                {
                    BuildTocLinks(sb, child, ref index, depth + 1);
                }
            }
        }
        
        /// <summary>
        /// Collect TOC entries into a flat list for column layout
        /// </summary>
        private static void CollectTocEntries(List<(string tocId, string title, int depth)> entries, OperationalDetail detail, int depth = 0)
        {
            if (!string.IsNullOrEmpty(detail.tocId) && !string.IsNullOrEmpty(detail.title))
            {
                entries.Add((detail.tocId, detail.title, depth));
            }
            
            if (detail.children != null)
            {
                foreach (var child in detail.children)
                {
                    CollectTocEntries(entries, child, depth + 1);
                }
            }
        }
```

                const int MAX_ROWS = 8;
                int totalEntries = tocEntries.Count;
                int numColumns = (totalEntries + MAX_ROWS - 1) / MAX_ROWS; // Ceiling division
                numColumns = Math.Max(1, numColumns); // At least 1 column
                
                // Create horizontal container for columns
                var columnsGO = new GameObject("TOCColumns");
                columnsGO.transform.SetParent(tocGO.transform, false);
                
                var columnsLayout = columnsGO.AddComponent<UnityEngine.UI.HorizontalLayoutGroup>();

```
# Changelog

All notable changes to Stationpedia Ascended will be documented in this file.

## [0.3.0] - 2025-12-31

### 🤖 Comprehensive AIMeE Documentation
- **Full AIMeE Guide** - Added extensive documentation for the AIMeE (ThingRobot) companion robot
- **Mode Explanations** - Detailed descriptions for all AIMeE modes: None, Follow, MoveToTarget, Roam, Unload, PathToTarget, StorageFull
- **IC10 Programming Guide** - Complete guide for programming AIMeE with example code snippets
- **Navigation & Mining** - Documented pathfinding behavior, mining mechanics, and coordinate systems
- **Table of Contents** - Added navigable TOC with clickable links to each section

### 📑 Multi-Column Table of Contents
- **Column Layout** - TOC now displays in columns (max 8 rows per column) instead of a long vertical list
- **Dynamic Columns** - Automatically creates additional columns when entries exceed 8
- **Improved Readability** - Better use of horizontal space for guides with many sections

### 🎨 Vanilla Mode Default & Easter Egg Toggle
- **Vanilla by Default** - Mod now starts in vanilla Stationpedia styling mode
- **Header Toggle** - Click the "Stationpedia" header to toggle Ascended mode (easter egg)
- **Visual Feedback** - Header changes to "Stationpedia Ascended" with orange styling when enabled
- **Icon Swap** - Custom phoenix icon only appears in Ascended mode
- **Removed Book Button** - Simplified UI by removing the mode toggle button

### 🔧 Bug Fixes
- **Fixed JSON Syntax Error** - Resolved corrupted line in descriptions.json that broke tooltip parsing
- **Fixed Brace Mismatch** - Corrected code structure issues in icon replacement logic

### 📝 New Files
- `VanillaModeManager.cs` - Static manager for vanilla/ascended mode state
- `CategoryHeaderHandler.cs` - Handler for category header interactions

---

## [0.2.2] - 2025-12-30

```
================================================================================
STATIONPEDIA ASCENDED v0.3.0 - LLM INSTRUCTIONS FOR WRITING descriptions.json
================================================================================

You are helping write device documentation for the Stationeers game mod 
"Stationpedia Ascended". This mod enhances the in-game encyclopedia with 
custom tooltips, collapsible sections, table of contents, images, and videos.

================================================================================
FILE STRUCTURE
================================================================================

The descriptions.json file has this top-level structure:

{
  "version": "1.0",
  "devices": [ ... array of device entries ... ],
  "genericDescriptions": { ... global fallback descriptions ... }
}

================================================================================
DEVICE ENTRY FORMAT (Complete Reference)
================================================================================

{
  "deviceKey": "ThingStructureDeviceName",      // REQUIRED - exact prefab name
  "displayName": "Human Readable Name",          // Optional - for documentation

  // === PAGE DESCRIPTION OPTIONS (pick ONE or combine append/prepend) ===
  "pageDescription": "Replaces entire description",
  "pageDescriptionAppend": "Added to end of existing description",
  "pageDescriptionPrepend": "Added to beginning of existing description",

  // === TABLE OF CONTENTS (TOC) ===
  "generateToc": true,                           // Enable TOC panel at top
  "tocTitle": "Quick Navigation",                // Custom TOC title (default: "Contents")

  // === OPERATIONAL DETAILS (MUST BE AN ARRAY) ===
  "operationalDetails": [ ... ],                 // Main content sections
  "operationalDetailsTitleColor": "#FF7A18",     // Title color (hex)
  "operationalDetailsBackgroundColor": "#1A2B3C", // Section background (hex)

  // === TOOLTIP DESCRIPTIONS (Objects with keys) ===
  "logicDescriptions": { ... },
  "modeDescriptions": { ... },
  "slotDescriptions": { ... },
  "versionDescriptions": { ... },
  "memoryDescriptions": { ... }
}

================================================================================
OPERATIONALDETAILS - ALL SUPPORTED PROPERTIES
================================================================================

Each item in the operationalDetails array can have these properties:

BASIC CONTENT:
  "title"           - (string) Section heading
  "description"     - (string) Main text content with TMP rich text support
  "items"           - (array of strings) Bullet list (rendered with • prefix)
  "steps"           - (array of strings) Numbered list (rendered as 1. 2. 3.)
  "children"        - (array) Nested OperationalDetail objects for sub-sections

COLLAPSIBLE SECTIONS:
  "collapsible"     - (boolean) If true, renders as expandable/collapsible section
                      Default: false (renders as inline text)

TABLE OF CONTENTS:
  "tocId"           - (string) Unique ID for TOC linking. When set:
                      - Section appears in the Table of Contents
                      - Clicking TOC link scrolls to this section
                      - Works on both collapsible and non-collapsible sections

MEDIA:
  "imageFile"       - (string) Image filename in mod/images/ folder
                      Supports: PNG, JPG, GIF (first frame only)
  "videoFile"       - (string) Video filename in mod/images/ folder
                      Supports: MP4. Renders as embedded video player
  "youtubeUrl"      - (string) Full YouTube URL (e.g., "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=...")
                      Renders as clickable link that opens in system browser
  "youtubeLabel"    - (string) Custom label for YouTube link
                      Default: "Watch on YouTube"

STYLING:
  "backgroundColor" - (string) Custom hex color for this section's background
                      Format: "#RRGGBB" (e.g., "#1A2B3C")

================================================================================
COLLAPSIBLE VS INLINE CONTENT
================================================================================

COLLAPSIBLE (collapsible: true):
  - Renders as expandable StationpediaCategory with header bar
  - User can click to expand/collapse
  - Supports nested children that also render as collapsibles
  - Best for: Long content, optional reading, nested documentation

INLINE (collapsible: false or not set):
  - Renders as regular text directly in the parent section
  - Always visible, cannot be collapsed
  - Best for: Short content, important notes, simple lists

Example:
{
  "title": "This is collapsible",
  "collapsible": true,
  "description": "User must click to see this content"
}

{
  "title": "This is inline",
  "description": "This content is always visible"
}

================================================================================
TABLE OF CONTENTS (TOC) SYSTEM
================================================================================

To enable TOC for a device:

1. Set "generateToc": true on the device entry
2. Add "tocId" to each section you want in the TOC

The TOC panel:
  - Appears at the top of Operational Details
  - Shows clickable links to each tocId section
  - Automatically uses multi-column layout (max 8 rows per column)
  - Clicking a link scrolls to and expands that section

Example:
{
  "deviceKey": "ThingRobot",
  "generateToc": true,
  "tocTitle": "AIMeE Guide",
  "operationalDetails": [
    {
      "title": "Overview",
      "tocId": "overview",
      "collapsible": true,
      "description": "Introduction to AIMeE..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Modes",
      "tocId": "modes",
      "collapsible": true,
      "children": [
        {
          "title": "Follow Mode",
          "tocId": "mode-follow",
          "collapsible": true,
          "description": "AIMeE follows the player..."
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

================================================================================
NESTING EXAMPLE (Deep Structure)
================================================================================

"operationalDetails": [
  {
    "title": "Steel Smelting",
    "tocId": "steel",
    "collapsible": true,
    "description": "The most common furnace recipe.",
    "children": [
      {
        "title": "Requirements",
        "tocId": "steel-requirements",
        "collapsible": true,
        "items": [
          "Iron Ore + Coal Ore in 3:1 ratio",
          "Temperature above 900K",
          "Pressure above 100 kPa"
        ]
      },
      {
        "title": "Process",
        "tocId": "steel-process",
        "collapsible": true,
        "steps": [
          "Load iron ore and coal (3:1 ratio)",
          "Add volatiles ice (fuel) and oxite ice (oxidizer)",
          "Close the furnace door",
          "Activate the furnace",
          "Wait for Mode=1 or temperature 900K+",
          "Collect ingots from export slot"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "title": "Tips",
    "tocId": "tips",
    "collapsible": true,
    "items": [
      "Furnace handles extreme pressures safely",
      "On cold planets, use more fuel ice",
      "Lock furnace via logic to prevent accidents"
    ]
  }
]

================================================================================
MEDIA EXAMPLES
================================================================================

IMAGE:
{
  "title": "Wiring Diagram",
  "imageFile": "furnace_wiring.png",
  "description": "Connect power as shown above."
}

VIDEO (Embedded MP4):
{
  "title": "Video Tutorial",
  "collapsible": true,
  "videoFile": "furnace_tutorial.mp4",
  "description": "Watch the full smelting process."
}

YOUTUBE LINK:
{
  "title": "External Tutorial",
  "collapsible": true,
  "youtubeUrl": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ABC123",
  "youtubeLabel": "Furnace Guide by StationeersGuru",
  "description": "Comprehensive video guide."
}

================================================================================
TEXTMESHPRO RICH TEXT FORMATTING
================================================================================

The mod uses TextMeshPro for rendering. Supported tags:

BASIC:
  <b>bold</b>
  <i>italic</i>
  <u>underline</u>
  <s>strikethrough</s>

COLORS:
  <color=#FF0000>hex color</color>
  <color=red>named color</color>

SIZE:
  <size=150%>bigger</size>
  <size=80%>smaller</size>
  <size=24>24pt</size>

ALIGNMENT:
  <align=center>centered</align>
  <align=right>right-aligned</align>

INDENTATION:
  <indent=10%>indented text</indent>

SUPER/SUBSCRIPT:
  H<sub>2</sub>O  →  H₂O
  x<sup>2</sup>   →  x²

OTHER:
  <nobr>no line break</nobr>
  <lowercase>LOWERCASE</lowercase>
  <uppercase>uppercase</uppercase>

LINE BREAKS:
  Use \n in JSON strings

================================================================================
TOOLTIP DESCRIPTIONS FORMAT
================================================================================

LOGIC DESCRIPTIONS:
"logicDescriptions": {
  "Temperature": {
    "dataType": "Float",
    "range": "0+",
    "description": "Internal temperature in Kelvin."
  },
  "Mode": {
    "dataType": "Integer",
    "range": "0-5",
    "description": "Current operational mode."
  }
}

MODE DESCRIPTIONS:
"modeDescriptions": {
  "Mode0": {
    "modeValue": "0",
    "description": "Device is idle/off."
  },
  "Mode1": {
    "modeValue": "1",
    "description": "Standard operation."
  }
}

SLOT DESCRIPTIONS:
"slotDescriptions": {
  "InputSlot": {
    "slotType": "Ore",
    "description": "Accepts ore for processing."
  }
}

VERSION DESCRIPTIONS:
"versionDescriptions": {
  "Tier1": {
    "description": "Basic model with limited capacity."
  }
}

MEMORY DESCRIPTIONS:
"memoryDescriptions": {
  "Setting": {
    "opCode": "s",
    "parameters": "value",
    "description": "Stores configuration value.",
    "byteLayout": "4 bytes"
  }
}

================================================================================
GENERIC DESCRIPTIONS (Fallback)
================================================================================

"genericDescriptions": {
  "logic": {
    "Power": "Device power state (0=off, 1=on)",
    "Setting": "Configurable parameter value"
  },
  "slotTypes": {
    "Ore": "Accepts raw ore items"
  },
  "slots": {
    "InputSlot": "Primary input for materials"
  },
  "modes": {
    "0": "Idle state",
    "1": "Active state"
  },
  "versions": {
    "Tier1": "Basic variant"
  },
  "connections": {
    "Power": "Electrical power connection"
  },
  "memory": {
    "Setting": {
      "opCode": "s",
      "parameters": "value",
      "description": "Configuration storage"
    }
  },
  "properties": {
    "MaxPressure": {
      "type": "Float",
      "description": "Maximum pressure tolerance"
    }
  }
}

================================================================================
COMPLETE DEVICE EXAMPLE
================================================================================

{
  "deviceKey": "ThingStructureFurnace",
  "displayName": "Furnace",
  "generateToc": true,
  "tocTitle": "Furnace Guide",
  "operationalDetailsTitleColor": "#FF7A18",
  "logicDescriptions": {
    "Temperature": {
      "dataType": "Float",

}

================================================================================
VALIDATION CHECKLIST
================================================================================

Before submitting your JSON:

[ ] deviceKey matches exact prefab name (e.g., "ThingStructureFurnace")
[ ] operationalDetails is an ARRAY with square brackets []
[ ] Each collapsible section has "collapsible": true
[ ] tocId values are unique within the device
[ ] generateToc is set to true if using tocId entries
[ ] Rich text tags are properly closed
[ ] Image/video files exist in mod/images/ folder
[ ] YouTube URLs are full URLs (not shortened)
[ ] JSON is valid (no trailing commas, proper brackets)
[ ] Nesting depth is reasonable (2-3 levels max recommended)

================================================================================
COMMON MISTAKES TO AVOID
================================================================================

WRONG - operationalDetails as object:
  "operationalDetails": { "title": "..." }

CORRECT - operationalDetails as array:
  "operationalDetails": [ { "title": "..." } ]

WRONG - Missing collapsible for expandable sections:
  { "title": "Section", "children": [...] }

CORRECT - Explicit collapsible:
  { "title": "Section", "collapsible": true, "children": [...] }

WRONG - tocId without generateToc:
  "operationalDetails": [ { "tocId": "section1" } ]

CORRECT - Enable TOC first:
  "generateToc": true,
  "operationalDetails": [ { "tocId": "section1" } ]

================================================================================
END OF INSTRUCTIONS
================================================================================

```
================================================================================
STATIONPEDIA ASCENDED v0.3.0 - LLM INSTRUCTIONS FOR WRITING descriptions.json
================================================================================

You are helping write device documentation for the Stationeers game mod 
"Stationpedia Ascended". This mod enhances the in-game encyclopedia with 
custom tooltips, collapsible sections, table of contents, images, and videos.

================================================================================
FILE STRUCTURE
================================================================================

The descriptions.json file has this top-level structure:

{
  "version": "1.0",
  "devices": [ ... array of device entries ... ],
  "genericDescriptions": { ... global fallback descriptions ... }
}

================================================================================
DEVICE ENTRY FORMAT (Complete Reference)
================================================================================

{
  "deviceKey": "ThingStructureDeviceName",      // REQUIRED - exact prefab name
  "displayName": "Human Readable Name",          // Optional - for documentation

  // === PAGE DESCRIPTION OPTIONS (pick ONE or combine append/prepend) ===
  "pageDescription": "Replaces entire description",
  "pageDescriptionAppend": "Added to end of existing description",
  "pageDescriptionPrepend": "Added to beginning of existing description",

  // === TABLE OF CONTENTS (TOC) ===
  "generateToc": true,                           // Enable TOC panel at top
  "tocTitle": "Quick Navigation",                // Custom TOC title (default: "Contents")

  // === OPERATIONAL DETAILS (MUST BE AN ARRAY) ===
  "operationalDetails": [ ... ],                 // Main content sections
  "operationalDetailsTitleColor": "#FF7A18",     // Title color (hex)
  "operationalDetailsBackgroundColor": "#1A2B3C", // Section background (hex)

  // === TOOLTIP DESCRIPTIONS (Objects with keys) ===
  "logicDescriptions": { ... },
  "modeDescriptions": { ... },
  "slotDescriptions": { ... },
  "versionDescriptions": { ... },
  "memoryDescriptions": { ... }
}

================================================================================
OPERATIONALDETAILS - ALL SUPPORTED PROPERTIES
================================================================================

Each item in the operationalDetails array can have these properties:

BASIC CONTENT:
  "title"           - (string) Section heading
  "description"     - (string) Main text content with TMP rich text support
  "items"           - (array of strings) Bullet list (rendered with • prefix)
  "steps"           - (array of strings) Numbered list (rendered as 1. 2. 3.)
  "children"        - (array) Nested OperationalDetail objects for sub-sections

COLLAPSIBLE SECTIONS:
  "collapsible"     - (boolean) If true, renders as expandable/collapsible section
                      Default: false (renders as inline text)

TABLE OF CONTENTS:
  "tocId"           - (string) Unique ID for TOC linking. When set:
                      - Section appears in the Table of Contents
                      - Clicking TOC link scrolls to this section
                      - Works on both collapsible and non-collapsible sections

MEDIA:
  "imageFile"       - (string) Image filename in mod/images/ folder
                      Supports: PNG, JPG, GIF (first frame only)
  "videoFile"       - (string) Video filename in mod/images/ folder
                      Supports: MP4. Renders as embedded video player
  "youtubeUrl"      - (string) Full YouTube URL (e.g., "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=...")
                      Renders as clickable link that opens in system browser
  "youtubeLabel"    - (string) Custom label for YouTube link
                      Default: "Watch on YouTube"

STYLING:
  "backgroundColor" - (string) Custom hex color for this section's background
                      Format: "#RRGGBB" (e.g., "#1A2B3C")

================================================================================
COLLAPSIBLE VS INLINE CONTENT
================================================================================

COLLAPSIBLE (collapsible: true):
  - Renders as expandable StationpediaCategory with header bar
  - User can click to expand/collapse
  - Supports nested children that also render as collapsibles
  - Best for: Long content, optional reading, nested documentation

INLINE (collapsible: false or not set):
  - Renders as regular text directly in the parent section
  - Always visible, cannot be collapsed
  - Best for: Short content, important notes, simple lists

Example:
{
  "title": "This is collapsible",
  "collapsible": true,
  "description": "User must click to see this content"
}

{
  "title": "This is inline",
  "description": "This content is always visible"
}

================================================================================
TABLE OF CONTENTS (TOC) SYSTEM
================================================================================

To enable TOC for a device:

1. Set "generateToc": true on the device entry
2. Add "tocId" to each section you want in the TOC

The TOC panel:
  - Appears at the top of Operational Details
  - Shows clickable links to each tocId section
  - Automatically uses multi-column layout (max 8 rows per column)
  - Clicking a link scrolls to and expands that section

Example:
{
  "deviceKey": "ThingRobot",
  "generateToc": true,
  "tocTitle": "AIMeE Guide",
  "operationalDetails": [
    {
      "title": "Overview",
      "tocId": "overview",
      "collapsible": true,
      "description": "Introduction to AIMeE..."
    },
    {
      "title": "Modes",
      "tocId": "modes",
      "collapsible": true,
      "children": [
        {
          "title": "Follow Mode",
          "tocId": "mode-follow",
          "collapsible": true,
          "description": "AIMeE follows the player..."
        }
      ]
    }
  ]
}

================================================================================
NESTING EXAMPLE (Deep Structure)
================================================================================

"operationalDetails": [
  {
    "title": "Steel Smelting",
    "tocId": "steel",
    "collapsible": true,
    "description": "The most common furnace recipe.",
    "children": [
      {
        "title": "Requirements",
        "tocId": "steel-requirements",
        "collapsible": true,
        "items": [
          "Iron Ore + Coal Ore in 3:1 ratio",
          "Temperature above 900K",
          "Pressure above 100 kPa"
        ]
      },
      {
        "title": "Process",
        "tocId": "steel-process",
        "collapsible": true,
        "steps": [
          "Load iron ore and coal (3:1 ratio)",
          "Add volatiles ice (fuel) and oxite ice (oxidizer)",
          "Close the furnace door",
          "Activate the furnace",
          "Wait for Mode=1 or temperature 900K+",
          "Collect ingots from export slot"
        ]
      }
    ]
  },
  {
    "title": "Tips",
    "tocId": "tips",
    "collapsible": true,
    "items": [
      "Furnace handles extreme pressures safely",
      "On cold planets, use more fuel ice",
      "Lock furnace via logic to prevent accidents"
    ]
  }
]

================================================================================
MEDIA EXAMPLES
================================================================================

IMAGE:
{
  "title": "Wiring Diagram",
  "imageFile": "furnace_wiring.png",
  "description": "Connect power as shown above."
}

VIDEO (Embedded MP4):
{
  "title": "Video Tutorial",
  "collapsible": true,
  "videoFile": "furnace_tutorial.mp4",
  "description": "Watch the full smelting process."
}

YOUTUBE LINK:
{
  "title": "External Tutorial",
  "collapsible": true,
  "youtubeUrl": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=ABC123",
  "youtubeLabel": "Furnace Guide by StationeersGuru",
  "description": "Watch a complete walkthrough."
}

================================================================================
TEXTMESHPRO RICH TEXT FORMATTING
================================================================================

The mod uses TextMeshPro for rendering. Supported tags:

BASIC:
  <b>bold</b>
  <i>italic</i>
  <u>underline</u>
  <s>strikethrough</s>

COLORS:
  <color=#FF0000>hex color</color>
  <color=red>named color</color>

SIZE:
  <size=150%>bigger</size>
  <size=80%>smaller</size>
  <size=24>24pt</size>

ALIGNMENT:
  <align=center>centered</align>
  <align=right>right-aligned</align>

INDENTATION:
  <indent=10%>indented text</indent>

SUPER/SUBSCRIPT:
  H<sub>2</sub>O  →  H₂O
  x<sup>2</sup>   →  x²

OTHER:
  <nobr>no line break</nobr>
  <lowercase>LOWERCASE</lowercase>
  <uppercase>uppercase</uppercase>

LINE BREAKS:
  Use \n in JSON strings

================================================================================
TOOLTIP DESCRIPTIONS FORMAT
================================================================================

LOGIC DESCRIPTIONS:
"logicDescriptions": {
  "Temperature": {
    "dataType": "Float",
    "range": "0+",
    "description": "Internal temperature in Kelvin."
  },
  "Mode": {
    "dataType": "Integer",
    "range": "0-5",
    "description": "Current operational mode."
  }
}

MODE DESCRIPTIONS:
"modeDescriptions": {
  "Mode0": {
    "modeValue": "0",
    "description": "Device is idle/off."
  },
  "Mode1": {
    "modeValue": "1",
    "description": "Standard operation."
  }
}

SLOT DESCRIPTIONS:
"slotDescriptions": {
  "InputSlot": {
    "slotType": "Ore",
    "description": "Accepts ore for processing."
  }
}

VERSION DESCRIPTIONS:
"versionDescriptions": {
  "Tier1": {
    "description": "Basic model with limited capacity."
  }
}

MEMORY DESCRIPTIONS:
"memoryDescriptions": {
  "Setting": {
    "opCode": "s",
    "parameters": "value",
    "description": "Stores configuration value.",
    "byteLayout": "4 bytes"
  }
}

================================================================================
GENERIC DESCRIPTIONS (Fallback)
================================================================================

"genericDescriptions": {
  "logic": {
    "Power": "Device power state (0=off, 1=on)",
    "Setting": "Configurable parameter value"
  },
  "slotTypes": {
    "Ore": "Accepts raw ore items"
  },
  "slots": {
    "InputSlot": "Primary input for materials"
  },
  "modes": {
    "0": "Idle state",
    "1": "Active state"
  },
  "versions": {
    "Tier1": "Basic variant"
  },
  "connections": {
    "Power": "Electrical power connection"
  },
  "memory": {
    "Setting": {
      "opCode": "s",
      "parameters": "value",
      "description": "Configuration storage"
    }
  },
  "properties": {
    "MaxPressure": {
      "type": "Float",
      "description": "Maximum pressure tolerance"
    }
  }
}

================================================================================
COMPLETE DEVICE EXAMPLE
================================================================================

================================================================================
VALIDATION CHECKLIST
================================================================================

Before submitting your JSON:

[ ] deviceKey matches exact prefab name (e.g., "ThingStructureFurnace")
[ ] operationalDetails is an ARRAY with square brackets []
[ ] Each collapsible section has "collapsible": true
[ ] tocId values are unique within the device
[ ] generateToc is set to true if using tocId entries
[ ] Rich text tags are properly closed
[ ] Image/video files exist in mod/images/ folder
[ ] YouTube URLs are full URLs (not shortened)
[ ] JSON is valid (no trailing commas, proper brackets)
[ ] Nesting depth is reasonable (2-3 levels max recommended)

================================================================================
COMMON MISTAKES TO AVOID
================================================================================

WRONG - operationalDetails as object:
  "operationalDetails": { "title": "..." }

CORRECT - operationalDetails as array:
  "operationalDetails": [ { "title": "..." } ]

WRONG - Missing collapsible for expandable sections:
  { "title": "Section", "children": [...] }

CORRECT - Explicit collapsible:
  { "title": "Section", "collapsible": true, "children": [...] }

WRONG - tocId without generateToc:
  "operationalDetails": [ { "tocId": "section1" } ]

CORRECT - Enable TOC first:
  "generateToc": true,
  "operationalDetails": [ { "tocId": "section1" } ]

================================================================================
END OF INSTRUCTIONS
================================================================================

```
## [0.3.0] - 2025-12-31

### 🤖 Comprehensive AIMeE Documentation
- **Full AIMeE Guide** - Added extensive documentation for the AIMeE (ThingRobot) companion robot
- **Mode Explanations** - Detailed descriptions for all AIMeE modes: None, Follow, MoveToTarget, Roam, Unload, PathToTarget, StorageFull
- **IC10 Programming Guide** - Complete guide for programming AIMeE with example code snippets
- **Navigation & Mining** - Documented pathfinding behavior, mining mechanics, and coordinate systems
- **Table of Contents** - Added navigable TOC with clickable links to each section

### 📑 Multi-Column Table of Contents
- **Column Layout** - TOC now displays in columns (max 8 rows per column) instead of a long vertical list
- **Dynamic Columns** - Automatically creates additional columns when entries exceed 8
- **Improved Readability** - Better use of horizontal space for guides with many sections

### 📋 New JSON Schema Features
New properties for building rich, interactive documentation:

**Device-Level Properties:**
- `generateToc` - (boolean) Enable Table of Contents panel at top of Operational Details
- `tocTitle` - (string) Custom title for TOC panel (default: "Contents")
- `operationalDetailsBackgroundColor` - (string) Hex color for section backgrounds

**OperationalDetail Properties:**
- `collapsible` - (boolean) Render as expandable/collapsible section with header bar
- `tocId` - (string) Unique ID for TOC linking; clicking scrolls to section
- `imageFile` - (string) Display inline image from mod/images/ folder
- `videoFile` - (string) Embed MP4 video player from mod/images/ folder
- `youtubeUrl` - (string) Clickable YouTube link that opens in browser
- `youtubeLabel` - (string) Custom label for YouTube link
- `backgroundColor` - (string) Custom hex color for individual section

### 🎨 Vanilla Mode Default & Easter Egg Toggle
- **Vanilla by Default** - Mod now starts in vanilla Stationpedia styling mode
- **Header Toggle** - Click the "Stationpedia" header to toggle Ascended mode (easter egg)
- **Visual Feedback** - Header changes to "Stationpedia Ascended" with orange styling when enabled
- **Icon Swap** - Custom phoenix icon only appears in Ascended mode
- **Removed Book Button** - Simplified UI by removing the mode toggle button

### 🔧 Bug Fixes
- **Fixed JSON Syntax Error** - Resolved corrupted line in descriptions.json that broke tooltip parsing
- **Fixed Brace Mismatch** - Corrected code structure issues in icon replacement logic

### 📝 New Files
- `VanillaModeManager.cs` - Static manager for vanilla/ascended mode state
- `CategoryHeaderHandler.cs` - Handler for category header interactions
- `LLM_INSTRUCTIONS.txt` - Completely rewritten documentation for JSON schema
```

## ⚙️ Configuration

### descriptions.json Schema

The mod uses a powerful JSON configuration system for creating rich documentation.

#### Device Entry Structure
```json
{
  "deviceKey": "ThingStructureFurnace",
  "displayName": "Furnace",
  
  "generateToc": true,
  "tocTitle": "Furnace Guide",
  
  "pageDescription": "Replaces entire description",
  "pageDescriptionAppend": "Added to end",
  "pageDescriptionPrepend": "Added to beginning",
  
  "operationalDetailsTitleColor": "#FF7A18",
  "operationalDetailsBackgroundColor": "#1A2B3C",
  
  "operationalDetails": [ ... ],
  "logicDescriptions": { ... },
  "modeDescriptions": { ... },
  "slotDescriptions": { ... }
}
```

#### Operational Details Properties
Each section in `operationalDetails` supports:

| Property | Type | Description |
|----------|------|-------------|
| `title` | string | Section heading |
| `description` | string | Main text content (supports TMP rich text) |
| `items` | array | Bullet list (• prefix) |
| `steps` | array | Numbered list (1. 2. 3.) |
| `children` | array | Nested sub-sections |
| `collapsible` | boolean | Make section expandable/collapsible |
| `tocId` | string | ID for Table of Contents linking |
| `imageFile` | string | Image from mod/images/ folder |
| `videoFile` | string | Embedded MP4 video player |
| `youtubeUrl` | string | Clickable YouTube link |
| `youtubeLabel` | string | Custom YouTube link text |
| `backgroundColor` | string | Hex color for section background |

#### Example: Full Device Entry
```json
{
  "deviceKey": "ThingStructureFurnace",
  "generateToc": true,
  "tocTitle": "Furnace Guide",
  "operationalDetails": [
    {
      "title": "Overview",
      "tocId": "overview",
      "collapsible": true,
      "description": "The Furnace smelts ores into ingots.",
      "imageFile": "furnace_diagram.png"
    },
    {
      "title": "Steel Smelting",
      "tocId": "steel",
      "collapsible": true,
      "children": [
        {
          "title": "Requirements",
          "tocId": "steel-reqs",
          "collapsible": true,
          "items": [
            "Iron Ore + Coal in 3:1 ratio",
            "Temperature above 900K"
          ]
        },
        {
          "title": "Process",
          "tocId": "steel-steps",
          "collapsible": true,
          "steps": [
            "Load iron ore and coal",
            "Add fuel ice",
            "Close door and activate"
          ]
        }
      ]
    },
    {
      "title": "Video Tutorial",
      "tocId": "video",
      "collapsible": true,
      "youtubeUrl": "https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=EXAMPLE",
      "youtubeLabel": "Watch Tutorial"
    }
  ],
  "logicDescriptions": {
    "Temperature": {
      "dataType": "Float",
      "range": "0+",
      "description": "Internal temperature in Kelvin."
    }
  }
}
```

#### Generic Descriptions (Fallback)
```json
{
  "genericDescriptions": {
    "logic": {
      "Power": "Device power state (0=off, 1=on)",
      "Setting": "Configurable parameter value"
    },
    "slots": {
      "InputSlot": "Primary input for materials"
    },
    "modes": {
      "0": "Idle state",
      "1": "Active state"
    }
  }
}
```

### LLM Instructions
For AI-assisted documentation writing, see `mod/LLM_INSTRUCTIONS.txt` which contains the complete schema reference and examples.

### descriptions.json
The mod uses a JSON configuration file for all customizations:

```json
{
  "genericDescriptions": {
    "logic": {
      "Power": "Controls device power state - 1 for on, 0 for off",
      "Setting": "Adjusts device operational parameters",
      "Temperature": "Current temperature reading in Kelvin"
    },
    "slots": {
      "Power": "Electrical power connection - provides or consumes electricity",
      "Atmosphere": "Gas input/output for atmospheric processing"
    },
    "memory": {
      "Setting": "Stores device configuration values",
      "Temperature": "Current temperature sensor reading"
    },
    "modes": {
      "0": "Device disabled/off state",
      "1": "Standard operational mode"
    },
    "connections": {
      "Power": "Electrical power delivery system",
      "Data": "Digital communication channel"
    }
  },
  "devices": {

      "operationalDetails": [
        {"header": "Pressure Management", "body": "This device can process multiple gas types simultaneously and requires careful pressure management."}

      "pageDescriptionAppend": "Additional information appended to the existing description.",
      "pageDescriptionPrepend": "Important notice added before the existing description."
    }
  }
}
```

#### Adding Logic Tooltips
